prompts.name: simulate_basic_xenobot
prompts.description: Run a baseline xenobot simulation with two motor_x cells and two passive cells arranged in a symmetric cross, and describe the resulting locomotion pattern.

messages.content: Simulate a xenobot with two motor_x cells at (1,0) and (-1,0) and two passive cells at (0,1) and (0,-1). What does its path look like and how far does it travel?

prompts.name: compare_motor_configurations
prompts.description: Compare the locomotion of a xenobot with all motor_x cells versus one with equal numbers of motor_x and motor_y cells, to explore how cell-type ratio affects trajectory direction.

messages.content: I want to compare two xenobot designs. First, simulate a bot with four motor_x cells at (0,0), (1,0), (2,0), (3,0). Then simulate a second bot with two motor_x cells at (0,0) and (1,0) and two motor_y cells at (0,1) and (1,1). Compare the net displacements and trajectory directions of the two designs.

prompts.name: passive_cell_drag_study
prompts.description: Investigate how adding passive (structural) cells affects net displacement by comparing a lean motor-only design with a scaffold-heavy design.

messages.content: Help me understand the effect of passive cells on a xenobot's locomotion. Simulate a minimal bot with just two motor_x cells at (0,0) and (1,0), then simulate an equivalent bot that adds four passive cells at (0.5,1), (0.5,-1), (-0.5,0.5), (-0.5,-0.5) to act as a structural frame. How much does the added scaffold reduce displacement?

prompts.name: diagonal_locomotion_design
prompts.description: Design a xenobot that moves diagonally at roughly 45 degrees by balancing motor_x and motor_y cell counts.

messages.content: Design a xenobot intended to locomote diagonally at 45 degrees. Place three motor_x cells and three motor_y cells in a compact hexagonal arrangement and simulate its trajectory. Does the path stay close to the 45-degree diagonal?

prompts.name: reverse_engineer_target_displacement
prompts.description: Given a target displacement of approximately 0.5 au along the x-axis, find the minimum number of motor_x cells needed by running progressive simulations.

messages.content: I need a xenobot that achieves at least 0.5 au of displacement along the x-axis. Starting with a single motor_x cell at (0,0) and one passive cell at (0,1), simulate and check if the target is met. If not, keep adding motor_x cells at (1,0), (2,0), etc. until the displacement threshold is reached, and report the minimum cell count required.
